title: Governance-Induced Service Unavailability
claim: Independent verification was unavailable to the authorized workflow.

observed_evidence.detail: Independent verification capability existed with more than one admissible authority-preserving route (independent, exact-bytes, read-only, attributable, transport-complete).

The pre-cleanup instruction surfaces made a specific stale MCP dependency mandatory and forbade the alternative: 'Run advisory verification via run_advisory_verifier only; never call the verifier directly', 'MCP/tooling unavailable -> MCP_UNAVAILABLE_HOLD', and 'If MCP is unavailable: hold and report the gap. Do not bypass the workflow or self-route manually.'

Cleanup Episode A (cap-processor 5a5b750, v5.com.ua 3069ad2, Concept-Arena ddad2d0) restored tool-agnostic admissible routing while preserving independence and evidence requirements: 'TOOL_UNAVAILABLE != WORK_SEMANTICALLY_INVALID' and 'independent verification may use any authorized available route that preserves independence, exact-byte identity, read-only behavior, attributable output, and complete transport.'

missing_evidence.detail: No evidence establishes that the mandatory MCP dependency was uniquely required by any safety or independence invariant; the postimage preserves those invariants through other admissible routes.
No evidence establishes that every admissible authority-preserving route was unavailable; an alternative route existed and was forbidden by policy rather than absent.

required_action: Establish that the mandatory dependency is not uniquely required, then restore an admissible authority-preserving path to the existing capability (any authorized route that preserves independence, exact-byte identity, read-only behavior where required, attributable output, and complete transport). Do not invent a new route, simulate the missing tool, or weaken an invariant the dependency legitimately protects, and do not accept the false 'capability unavailable' conclusion.
notes: Historical classification: VERIFIED_INTERNAL_HISTORICAL_SCOPE (narrow) for the withholding predicate only. Primary evidence is Cleanup Episode A (the 'chore(ai): remove stale cap-probe governance' commits cap-processor 5a5b750, v5.com.ua 3069ad2, Concept-Arena ddad2d0), whose exact preimage made a specific stale MCP dependency mandatory and mandated withholding-on-unavailability while forbidding the admissible alternative, and whose postimage restored tool-agnostic admissible routing. NOT claimed as fact: any particular per-episode unavailability timestamp; that the underlying provider itself was ever down; any request id or model call. Negative controls that must NOT be read as this incident: a genuine external provider outage; a case where no admissible alternative path exists (service legitimately unavailable); a dependency uniquely required by a verified safety or independence invariant; a transient transport failure that was correctly held and reported as an infrastructure terminal without granting a false service-unavailable conclusion. This is not an authority-blind gateway decision (AICE-602), not a self-hosting mutation deadlock where installing the capability needs the same missing capability (AICE-613), and not an infrastructure failure fabricated into a semantic verdict (AICE-614): it is an existing capability withheld because an unnecessary broken governance dependency was mandatory over an available admissible route.
